main.description: Plan sealed-archive downloads for a historical backfill. The response names whole sealed segments or sealed-segment block ranges that may contain rows matching the requested exact DID and collection filters. This is a transport planner only: clients must still decode rows, apply exact filtering, and coordinate live-tail subscription independently. On the first page (wantDidTombstones), the response also carries a DID-level tombstone snapshot so a collection-filtered backfill can suppress records of accounts deleted within the planned range.

dids.description: Exact DIDs to match. Missing or empty means all DIDs.

collections.description: Collection filters to match. Each entry is either an exact NSID (e.g. app.bsky.feed.post) or a namespace wildcard ending in '.*' (e.g. app.bsky.feed.*), which matches every collection under that namespace prefix. Missing or empty means all collections. A wildcard that matches no archived collection yields an empty plan (it is not treated as match-all).

afterSeq.description: Lower exclusive sequence bound. Rows with seq <= afterSeq are outside the requested window.

beforeSeq.description: Upper inclusive sequence bound. Rows with seq > beforeSeq are outside the requested window.

wantDidTombstones.description: Set true ONLY on the first page of a fresh backfill to request the DID-level tombstone snapshot over (afterSeq, plannedThroughSeq]. A collection-filtered backfill never downloads account-delete/sync markers (they carry an empty collection and ride in no collection block), so without this snapshot a folding consumer would keep a deleted account's records forever. DID-level only; record-level tombstones ride inline through the normal download. Subsequent pages omit this.

plannedThroughSeq.description: Highest sealed seq covered by this plan, capped by beforeSeq when provided.

didTombstonesIncluded.description: True iff this response carries the DID-tombstone snapshot (i.e. wantDidTombstones was set on the request and the server honored it). The client MUST gate on this flag, not on didTombstones being non-empty: an empty snapshot is a legitimate result, but a missing snapshot from a too-old server must be treated as a fatal fail-closed condition (proceeding with an empty suppression set would silently retain deleted accounts' records).

didTombstones.description: DID-level tombstones (account-delete / sync) with seq in (afterSeq, plannedThroughSeq], intersected with the request's DID filter when one is set. Present only when didTombstonesIncluded is true. The client folds these as seq-scoped, DID-only suppression of its own emitted create/update rows (drop a row iff its DID's tombstone seq is strictly greater than the row's seq); it MUST NOT synthesize delete/account events from them.

errors.name: PlanTooLarge
errors.description: The plan would exceed the server's configured response/work entry limit.

name.description: Segment filename accepted by getSegment and getBlock.

checksum.description: Segment-format xxh3 metadata checksum as 16-char hex.

mode.description: segment means download the whole segment with getSegment; blocks means download the listed block ranges with getBlock.

blocks.description: Inclusive block ranges. Present only when mode is blocks.

first.description: First block index in this inclusive range.

last.description: Last block index in this inclusive range.

did.description: The account whose DID-level tombstone (account-delete or sync) this is.

seq.description: The jetstream seq of the highest account-delete/sync for this DID within the snapshot range. The client drops its own emitted create/update rows for this DID with a strictly smaller seq.

entries.description: Number of response work entries counted against the server plan limit.
